tweets.text: The best part, in my mind, is having Things on the apple watch, because I can quickly pull up my to-do list, and have my upcoming to-dos on the homescreen so it stares me in the face, pretty close to writing something on the back of your hand (but it's synced) [not my screenshot] https://t.co/ZwTiHIi8WS

Here are the tags I use, most-used are the time tags. If I tag something 5 or 10 minutes when I make the task, it pushes me to do it later when I'm scanning. Also when I only have a set amount of time to complete a task I can filter to just <20m, or filter 1hr if I have more time https://t.co/EkfP6BYYHF

@dataeditor I used to use Wunderlist before I found Things, I think they have an Android app, but I only give them a shrugging endorsement as opposed to full-fledged love for Things https://t.co/IX41biddmD

This is very personal and I don't think I've ever done this before, but others might find this interesting, this is what my to-do list looks like on an average day (please also note it is 10pm and none have been completed) https://t.co/ygIKApMvWM

@dataeditor I've been using https://t.co/AJuMFpZ4dG on iPhone / iOS / Apple Watch for as long as I can remember, quickly add things as they come up, organize in bed in the morning https://t.co/Dpr0kCmOHf
@bluechoochoo I keep my browser window at ~900px width so I didn't even notice it had a bigger version, which, uggs, no thanks

I think I'm done coding my Generative Music series, going from completely randomized notes to choosing random scales & chords from Tonal.js and sampling an audio file https://t.co/eK9NOqe0WO - going back to refactor and add comments and then hopefully going to form into a blog https://t.co/i3zjxiXrpG

I think about that a lot now, as things "open up" – it never mattered, the experts still advise staying at home.

I keep thinking back to an interview with an anarchist nurse at the onset of the pandemic, when asked "how, as an anarchist can you obey a stay at home order?" and he basically said he isn't, he is listening to medical experts who advise staying at home.

This is handy, and @OpenAI make it really easy to copy copy playground prompts out into code. So I move over into vscode where @GitHubCopilot is waiting for me, and I tell it I want to add the responses to every tweet- co-pilot does a great job as always, GPT-3 integrating itself https://t.co/g8ZFuoQ1sU

So I really want to explore all of my bookmarks/tweets/etc by topic, but my tweets aren't tagged! I could start using hashtags, but what about my 10 years of tweets? So I figured, why not give GPT-3 a shot at adding tags to Tweets? It does a great job in my spot tests https://t.co/94oTtnOEws
